text: It is widely accepted that training deep neural networks successfully requires datasets with thousands of annotated samples. In this paper, we propose a novel network architecture and training strategy that leverages aggressive data augmentation to utilize available annotated samples more efficiently. The architecture features a symmetric design consisting of a contracting path to capture global context and an expanding path that enables precise localization. We demonstrate that this network can be trained end-to-end using very few images, outperforming the previous state-of-the-art—a sliding-window convolutional network—on the ISBI challenge for segmenting neuronal structures in electron microscopy stacks. Using the same architecture trained on transmitted light microscopy images (phase contrast and DIC), we won the 2015 ISBI cell tracking challenge in these categories by a significant margin. Furthermore, the network is highly efficient, segmenting a 512x512 image in less than one second on a modern GPU. Our full Caffe-based implementation and the pre-trained networks are available at http://lmb.informatik.uni-freiburg.de/people/ronneber/u-net.
